<!-- # Location {.unnumbered} -->

In the data model, a **Location** represents a general area where **Field Visits** take place. **Locations** are not defined by exact latitude and longitude coordinates (unlike **Events**, defined later), but they serve as a broader spatial grouping for organizing data. The meaning of a **Location** depends on the methodology of the Surveillance Activity. Users must define what a **Location** represents and document it in the **Surveillance Activity** metadata. 

<!-- A single Location can contain zero Events (e.g., when an area is surveyed but no **Event** is recorded) or multiple **Events** (e.g., multiple observations or findings within the same area). -->

<!-- What a **Location** represents depends on the methodology of the **Surveillance Activity**. **Users must define the specific unit a Location represents and document it in the Surveillance Activity metadata**. We strongly suggest to define a **Location** as a site that is explored to find **Events**.  -->

<!-- For *Arthropod Sources*, a **Location** may represent a parcel where traps are set. During ranger patrols, a **Location** might be defined as the entire protected area or a specific zone within it.  -->

Key attributes of a **Location** include:

* Location ID and Code
* Location Cross-Reference ID and Origin
* Location Type

Other attributes are provided in the [Data Dictionary](https://dmontecino.github.io/WH_Database/data_dictionary.html) (under construction)

::: {.callout-note title="Case Study 4"}

In the sampling of wildlife in markets, a Location can refer to a specific market

:::
